title: claytonia parviflora subsp. depressa
intro: Claytonia parviflora subsp. depressa, also known as the smallflower miner's lettuce, is a subspecies of the wild lettuce plant that belongs to the Portulacaceae family. It is a perennial herb with edible leaves that have been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Claytonia parviflora subsp. depressa include its ability to support digestion, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Claytonia parviflora subsp. depressa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salads, and is also used as a garnish or added to soups and other dishes for its nutritional value.
